description: Use this skill whenever the user wants to apply a verified fix or refactor — driven either by findings from `spec-audit` (conformance mode) / `robust-review` / `code-review`, OR by a free-form bug description — with a built-in safety net (build / type / test gate after each edit, automatic revert when the change breaks anything green). Trigger phrases include "fix the spec mismatches", "reconcile spec and implementation", "fix the robust-review findings", "auto-fix critical findings", "apply the unwrap fixes", "fix this bug safely", "fix and verify", "patch this without breaking anything", "remediate the security findings", or any pattern-based remediation request. Trigger even when the user does not say "safe-fix" — phrases like "fix what robust-review found", "close the gap between design and code", "make implementation match the spec", or "patch this and run the tests" qualify. For broad multi-component feature work, prefer `impl-orchestrator` instead.

results.query: Apply safe-fix Mode A on the 12 spec-audit conformance findings against src/order/ — let the heuristics decide direction (robustness for type drift, test existence for behavior, git blame for ambiguous Diverged).
spec-audit found 8 Constraint violations between DESIGN/02_payment.md and src/payment/. Reconcile them — spec wins for the constants, impl wins for the runtime behavior.
auto sync DESIGN/03_order.md and src/order/ pls. spec-audit conformance has a list ready, let safe-fix close the loop with the heuristics
Run safe-fix --mode=conformance --loop on the auth component until the diff hits zero. Cap at 5 iterations and verify after each pass.
Auto-fix the 5 SEC-Critical and 4 ROB-High findings from robust-review on src/auth/ — unwrap removal, SQL parameterization, the standard patterns. Verify with the test suite after each patch.
robust-review just dumped 14 findings (5 Critical, 4 High in src/payment/db.ts). Run the safe remediation pipeline with build/type/test gate between each patch.
patch all the high severity stuff from robust-review on src/api/. dont break anything, run tests after each patch and revert if green turns red
There's a bug in src/auth/login.ts:142 — when the OAuth provider returns null email, we crash. Fix it and verify all auth-related regression tests still pass after the fix.
I'm scared to refactor handlers/checkout.ts — too much hidden coupling. Apply the safe edit pipeline (fix → verify → revert if broken) when extracting the validateOrder helper.
small bug in formatDate at src/utils/date.ts:88, returning UTC instead of local. patch it but make sure nothing else breaks. heavy regression risk on date stuff
Make sure the JSON shape returned by GET /api/orders matches what the React frontend expects — generate a boundary contract test for /api/orders.
Light PR review on the diff in src/utils/ — Critical/High/Medium/Low ranking, no fixes yet, ~5 min max.
Run a deep security and robustness audit on src/auth/ — injection vectors, panic sources, race conditions. Critical/High breakdown only, no fixes yet.
Compare DESIGN/01_auth.md vs src/auth/ — just the diff, don't fix anything yet. I want to review the Missing/Diverged/Extra/Constraint list before applying.
Generate DESIGN/06_inventory.md from plans/INVENTORY.md — we don't have a design doc yet for this component.
Implement the entire chat component end-to-end from DESIGN/08_chat.md — code generation, verification gate, parallel reviews, the full autonomous loop.
Save my session state before /clear — capture progress, git state, decisions, and a hand-off note so I can pick this up tomorrow.
What's the best way to handle async errors in Node.js — global handlers vs try/catch wrapping?
Help me write a CONTRIBUTING.md for the project covering PR workflow and review expectations.
What's the difference between encoding and encryption? Need a 1-paragraph explanation for a junior dev.
